operator_instances.transformation_goal: Increase constraint complexity by requiring the version message to follow an exact, structured format with fixed line order and content, and by adding additional output checks in the test harness.
operator_instances.rationale: The current seed only checks for substring presence, not the exact output structure. Enforcing a precise line-by-line format raises the bar for correctness, making the function more constrained in its output. This aligns with Computation/Constraint Complexity.

required_capabilities: using sys and pandas and networkx

visible_state_patch.context_summary:
import sys
import types
import pandas as pd
import networkx as nx

# Create a dummy macsypy module for testing
macsypy = types.SimpleNamespace(__version__="1.2.3",
                                solution=types.SimpleNamespace(nx=nx),
                                __citation__="Doe et al., 2023")

<<insert solution here>>

def main():
    msg = get_version_message()
    print(msg)
    # Verify expected contents
    assert "Macsyfinder 1.2.3" in msg
    assert "- Python" in msg
    assert "- NetworkX" in msg
    assert "- Pandas" in msg
    assert "If you use this software please cite" in msg
    # Additional strict format checks
    lines = msg.strip().split('\n')
    assert lines[0] == "Macsyfinder 1.2.3"
    assert lines[1] == "using:"
    assert lines[2].startswith("- Python ")
    assert lines[3].startswith("- NetworkX ")
    assert lines[4].startswith("- Pandas ")
    assert lines[5] == ""
    assert lines[6] == "MacsyFinder is distributed under the terms of the GNU General Public License (GPLv3)."
    assert lines[7] == "See the COPYING file for details."
    assert lines[8] == ""
    assert lines[9] == "If you use this software please cite:"
    assert lines[10] == "Doe et al., 2023"
    assert lines[11] == "and don't forget to cite models used:"
    assert lines[12] == "macsydata cite <model>"

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

verifier_state_patch.verifier_type_hint: string assertion and exact line matching

hidden_tests.description: Check that the returned message matches the exact expected format line by line.

expected_failure_modes: If any line differs from expected, assertion fails.

semantic_test_specs.semantic_intent: Prove that the function returns a message with an exact, predetermined line structure.
semantic_test_specs.target_constraint: The output must consist of exactly 13 lines (when stripped) with specific content for each line, as defined in the visible assertions.
semantic_test_specs.expected_failure_mode: Old seed behavior that only ensures substring presence would pass but now fails if the format is misordered or extra/missing lines.
semantic_test_specs.test_case_description: The scaled execution case replays the same call but now includes additional strict output checks.

output_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include the exact line-by-line assertions shown in the updated main() function; gold solution must still pass.
